AlgebraicConstraint: Constrain input signal to zero and output algebraic state
ArrayProcessingSubsystem: Apply subsystem processing to each subarray of multidimensional signal
Assertion: Verify assertion condition or stop simulation
Assignment: Assign values to specified elements of input signal
AtomicSubsystem: Execute subsystem as single atomic unit
Backlash: Model system with play or backlash
Band-LimitedWhiteNoise: Generate band-limited white noise for continuous systems
Bias: Add constant bias to input signal
BitClear: Clear specified bit in stored integer
BitSet: Set specified bit in stored integer
BlockSupportTable: Specify code generation support for custom blocks
BusAssignment: Assign values to specified elements of bus signal
CFunction: Call external C function during simulation
CMBlock: Create custom block using MATLAB code
Checks_DGap: Verify discrete signal has no unexpected gaps between values
Checks_DMax: Verify discrete signal does not exceed maximum value
Checks_DMin: Verify discrete signal is not below minimum value
Checks_DRange: Verify discrete signal is within specified range
Checks_Gradient: Verify signal gradient is within bounds
Checks_SGap: Verify signal has no unexpected gaps between values
Checks_SMax: Verify signal does not exceed maximum value
Checks_SMin: Verify signal is not below minimum value
Checks_SRange: Verify signal is within specified range
CombinatorialLogic: Implement truth table for combinational logic
ComplexToMagnitudeAngle: Convert complex signal to magnitude and phase angle
ComplexToRealImag: Separate complex signal into real and imaginary parts
ComposeString: Create formatted string from input values
Concatenate: Concatenate input signals along specified dimension
ConversionInherited: Convert data type while inheriting signal properties
CoulombicandViscousFriction: Model combined Coulombic and viscous friction
CounterFree-Running: Count up indefinitely from initial value
CounterLimited: Count up with specified upper limit and wrap behavior
CrossProduct: Compute cross product of two 3-element vectors
DataStoreMemory: Define named data store for signal sharing
DataStoreRead: Read data from named data store
DataStoreWrite: Write data to named data store
DataTypeConversion: Convert input signal to specified data type
DataTypeDuplicate: Output signal with same data type as reference input
DataTypePropagation: Specify data type propagation for expression blocks
DeadZoneDynamic: Output zero when input is within dynamically specified range
DecrementTimeToZero: Decrement time value to zero at sample rate
Delay: Delay discrete-time input by specified number of samples
DetectChange: Output true when input value changes
DetectDecrease: Output true when input value decreases
DetectFallNegative: Output true when input crosses zero from positive to negative
DetectFallNonpositive: Output true when input crosses zero from positive to zero or negative
DetectIncrease: Output true when input value increases
DetectRiseNonnegative: Output true when input crosses zero from negative to zero or positive
DetectRisePositive: Output true when input crosses zero from negative to positive
Difference: Output difference between current and delayed input
DigitalClock: Output simulation time at specified sampling interval
DiscreteFilter: Implement IIR or FIR discrete-time filter
DiscreteZeroPole: Implement discrete-time transfer function specified by zeros, poles, and gain
Display: Display value of input signal during simulation
DocBlock: Display formatted text and images as block documentation
DotProduct: Compute scalar dot product of two vectors
EnablePort: Control execution of subsystem with external enable signal
EnabledAndTriggeredSubsystem: Execute subsystem when both enabled and triggered
EntityTransportDelay: Delay entity by specified transport time
EnumeratedConstant: Output constant value of enumerated type
ExpandScalar: Expand scalar to vector or matrix with repeated values
ExtractBits: Extract contiguous bits from stored integer
Find: Output indices and values of nonzero input elements
FirstOrderHold: Implement first-order sample-and-hold to convert discrete signal to continuous
Fixed-PointState-Space: Implement state-space system using fixed-point arithmetic
FloatExtractBits: Extract and reinterpret bits from floating-point signal
ForEachSubsystem: Repeat subsystem execution for each element, column, or subarray
ForIteratorSubsystem: Repeat subsystem execution specified number of times
FromWorkspace: Output data from MATLAB workspace variable
Function-CallSubsystem: Execute subsystem when function-call event occurs
FunctionCallFeedback: Create feedback path in function-call subsystem
FunctionCallGenerator: Generate function-call events at regular intervals
FunctionCallSplit: Execute multiple function-call subsystems from single function-call signal
FunctionCaller: Call Simulink function or exported Stateflow function
Goto: Send signal to From blocks without connecting lines
GotoTagVisibility: Define scope of Goto-From signal connection
Ground: Terminate unconnected input port with ground connection
HermitianTranspose: Compute conjugate transpose of complex matrix
HitScheduler: Schedule function-call subsystem execution at specified intervals
If: Route signals based on conditional expressions
IfActionSubsystem: Execute subsystem for If block condition
InitialCondition: Provide initial condition for signal feedback loop
IntervalTest: Check whether signal is in specified interval
IntervalTestDynamic: Check whether signal is in dynamically specified interval
LeadorLagCompensator: Implement continuous lead or lag compensator
Logic: Perform logical operation (AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, NXOR, NOT)
LookupNDDirect: Index into n-dimensional table using direct indexing
Lookup_n-D: Approximate n-dimensional function using lookup table
M-S-Function: Create S-function using MATLAB programming language
MATLABFcn: Apply MATLAB function or expression to input (deprecated, use MATLAB Function)
MATLABSystem: Use MATLAB System object in Simulink model
MagnitudeAngleToComplex: Convert magnitude and phase angle to complex signal
ManualSwitch: Switch between two inputs interactively during simulation
ManualVariantSink: Manually select which variant sink is active
ManualVariantSource: Manually select which variant source is active
Math: Perform mathematical function (exp, log, 10^u, magnitude^2, square, pow, reciprocal, hypot, rem, mod, transpose)
Memory: Output previous time step value of input
Merge: Combine multiple input signals into single output
MessageMerge: Merge messages from multiple message sources
MessagePollingSubsystem: Execute subsystem when message is polled
MessageTriggeredSubsystem: Execute subsystem when message is received
MinMax: Output minimum or maximum of inputs
MinMaxRunningResettable: Compute running minimum or maximum with reset
MultiPortSwitch: Select one of multiple inputs based on control signal
NeighborhoodProcessingSubsystem: Apply subsystem processing to neighborhoods of multidimensional signal
PID2dof: Implement two degree-of-freedom PID controller with setpoint weighting
PMIOPort: Connect physical modeling and Simulink signal domains
ParameterWriter: Write block parameter values during simulation
PermuteDimensions: Rearrange order of multidimensional signal dimensions
PixelProcessingSubsystem: Apply subsystem processing to pixels of multidimensional signal
PreLookup: Compute index and fraction values for interpolation
Probe: Output signal properties (width, sample time, complex signal flag)
PropagationDelay: Delay input by specified propagation time
PythonCode: Execute Python code in Simulink model
Quantizer: Discretize input signal to specified quantization interval
Ramp: Generate continuously increasing or decreasing signal
RateLimiterDynamic: Limit rate of change using dynamically specified bounds
RateTransition: Transfer data between blocks operating at different sample rates
RealImagToComplex: Combine real and imaginary inputs into complex signal
Receive: Receive message from Send block or message queue
Record: Record signal data to base workspace or file
Relay: Switch output between two values based on relay logic
ResetFunctionSubsystem: Execute subsystem when reset event occurs
ResettableSubsystem: Reset subsystem states to initial conditions when triggered
Reshape: Change dimensionality of signal without changing number of elements
Rounding: Round input to nearest integer using specified rounding method
SampleTimeMath: Adjust sample time of signal using mathematical operation
SaturationDynamic: Limit signal to dynamically specified upper and lower bounds
ScalingStrip: Remove scaling information from fixed-point signal
ScanString: Parse formatted string into output values
SecondOrderIntegrator: Implement second-order integration of input signal
Selector: Select subset of input elements by index
Send: Send message to Receive block or message queue
SignalConversion: Convert signal to new type for connection compatibility
SignalEditor: Create, edit, and switch between signal scenarios
SignalGenerator: Generate square, sawtooth, sine, or random waveform
SignalSpecification: Specify signal name, units, dimensions, data type, and other properties
SimulinkFunctionSubsystem: Define function that can be called from Simulink
SineandCosine: Compute sine and cosine of input simultaneously
SliderGain: Vary scalar gain using interactive slider
Squeeze: Remove dimensions of length 1 from signal
StateReader: Read state values from specified block
StateWriter: Write values to state of specified block
Step: Generate step between two levels at specified time
Stop: Stop simulation when input is nonzero
StoredIntegerValueDecrement: Decrement stored integer with wrap or saturation
StoredIntegerValueIncrement: Increment stored integer with wrap or saturation
StringCompare: Compare two strings for equality or ordering
StringConcatenate: Concatenate input strings into single string
StringContains: Determine whether string contains specified pattern
StringCount: Count occurrences of pattern in string
StringFind: Find starting index of pattern in string
StringToASCII: Convert string to uint8 vector of ASCII values
SubSystem: Group blocks into subsystem for model hierarchy
SwitchCase: Route input to cases based on case value
SwitchCaseActionSubsystem: Execute subsystem for Switch Case condition
TimedLinearization: Linearize model at specified simulation times
ToString: Convert input value to string representation
TransferFcnDirectFormII: Implement transfer function using direct form II structure
TransferFcnDirectFormIITransposed: Implement transfer function using transposed direct form II structure
TransferFcnRealZero: Implement transfer function with real zero
TransportDelay: Delay input by variable or fixed time
Transpose: Transpose rows and columns of matrix
TriggerPort: Control subsystem execution with external trigger signal
TriggeredLinearization: Linearize model when triggered during simulation
TriggeredSubsystem: Execute subsystem when triggered by rising, falling, or either edge
Trigonometry: Apply trigonometric function (sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, sinh, cosh, tanh, asinh, acosh, atanh)
UnitConversion: Convert signal from one unit to another
VariablePulseGenerator: Generate pulse with variable pulse width
VariantEnd: Mark end of variant region for code generation
VariantStart: Mark start of variant region for code generation
VariantSubsystem: Select variant subsystem configuration based on condition
VirtualSubsystem: Group blocks graphically without affecting execution or code generation
WhileIteratorSubsystem: Repeat subsystem execution while condition is true
Width: Output number of elements in input signal
WrapToZero: Set output to zero when input exceeds threshold
ZeroOrderHold: Sample and hold input at specified rate
ZeroPole: Implement continuous transfer function specified by zeros, poles, and gain
EventListener: Add event port and specify event type for Initialize, Reset, or Terminate function subsystems
Chart: Stateflow chart containing elements that implement state-based logic
StateTransitionTable: Represent state transitions in a tabular format; contents are traversable like a regular Stateflow chart with states and transitions
TruthTable: Evaluate logical conditions and execute actions based on combinations of true/false outcomes, serialized as conditions (C1, C2...) and decisions (D1, D2... with T/F/- outcomes)
